notable_excerpts: On our internal sustainability journey under the Project 'Alingana', we have made progress in all the three areas of decarbonization, circularity and preserving biodiversity.
We now have three plants certified for water neutrality, three plants, which have already achieved zero waste to landfill.
We launched AcePro EV in the last quarter and have had a very good response.
Electric mobility is a key focus area. We signed an MOU with green energy mobility solutions to supply 100 Magna EV intercity coaches.
Efforts on the central Asian flyway and elephant conservation are on track.
In our online sales platform, Fleetverse, we now have more than 22,000 platform assisted retails in the entire quarter two.
E-Dukaan, our online spare part sale, we now have 10,000 customers and 33,000 retailers.
We have been very prudent as majority of the spends are largely on decarbonization and circularity initiatives.
Prudent Capex spends, which are well within the guidance range that we had given of around 2% to 4% of revenue.
We passed the entire GST reduction benefit to customers through the price cuts.

chain_of_thought: The earnings call highlighted Tata Motors' strong focus on environmental sustainability through initiatives like decarbonization, circularity, and biodiversity conservation. Social aspects were addressed through community engagement and digital transformation efforts. Governance was emphasized through transparency in financial dealings and prudent risk management. The environmental pillar was the strongest, with specific achievements in water neutrality and waste management.
strengths: Commitment to transparency and prudent financial management.
weaknesses: Limited specific details on social initiatives.
